commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=186ba0d7a064164f0d123e6ae0cd9ebb490596b9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds recovery-language matching to embedded-agent payload warning suppression and tests recovered versus unresolved command-failure wording.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +35. Total +42 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main lacks the recovery acknowledgement shape, and the PR-introduced failure is source-reproducible from the new regex matching generic `error ... script ... fixed/passed` prose while `resolveToolErrorWarningPolicy` uses that boolean to hide mutating warnings.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] A narrow code repair is clear, but the external PR also needs contributor-provided real behavior proof, so this should stay in human review rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: [codex] Suppress recovered tool failure warnings
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

When an embedded agent finishes with a clear user-facing reply after recovering from a tool command failure, OpenClaw can still append a separate mutating-tool warning from the last failed command. That duplicate warning makes a recovered turn look broken even when the final assistant reply already explains the failed command and the successful recovery.

The existing warning policy already suppresses mutating-tool warnings when the assistant explicitly acknowledges that the action failed. It did not recognize the adjacent recovery shape: the assistant says a command/check failed, says it reran or corrected the command, and says the retry passed.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the fail-closed mutating-tool behavior intact. The shell mutation classifier still treats ambiguous commands, including pipelines, as mutating unless their structured contract proves otherwise. The only change is in the user-facing payload decision: an explicit failure-plus-recovery acknowledgement is now treated like the existing explicit failure acknowledgement.

The recovery pattern requires both a failure/action signal and a recovery outcome, so plain success copy such as "the command completed with no errors" still does not hide an unresolved mutating tool failure.

## User Impact

Users get fewer false terminal warnings after recovered command/check failures. Real mutating failures remain visible when the assistant merely claims success, uses no-op phrasing, or does not explicitly acknowledge the failed action/recovery.

## Evidence

Local validation on this branch:

```bash
corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
corepack pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test
git diff --check
env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm check:changed
```

Results:

- Focused payload regression test passed: 1 file, 56 tests.
- Adjacent payload/mutation tests passed: 3 files, 155 tests.
- Formatting, targeted oxlint, repo oxlint wrapper, core test typecheck, whitespace check, and child-mode changed gate passed.
